extraction.one_line_summary: WOUT van Aert won from the breakaway after dominating both Ventoux ascents, while VINGEGAARD attacked the yellow jersey group and cracked POGAČAR before being caught.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT attacked solo from breakaway on final Ventoux ascent gaining 4.5 minutes, proving grand tour stage versatility metric.
VINGEGAARD's white jersey attack exposed POGAČAR weakness before yellow jersey recovered on descent, revealing recovery pattern.
INEOS controlled early pace on first Ventoux ascent forcing break selection before attacks, showing double-climb pacing strategy.
Trek-Segafredo placed three riders in break (MOLLEMA, ELISSONDE) but couldn't counter VAN AERT solo power.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Led mountain stage 24 hours after sprint second place, demonstrating unprecedented versatility recovery between effort types.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: First visible crack when VINGEGAARD attacked, needed chase effort to limit losses, concerning sign mid-tour.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Attacked yellow jersey group and gained 30 seconds over summit, showing GC threat potential not just domestique role.
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: Suffered badly in heat and dropped from 2nd to 5th GC, losing over five minutes post-Alpine success.
rider_signals.GAUDU David: negative: Dropped early on easier Sault ascent despite sitting 10th GC, heat exposure weakness revealed.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Leading KOM competition but dropped on easier first Ventoux ascent, showing declining climbing form.
